description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two dark metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood is textured with visible grain, knots, and weathering, giving it a rustic, aged appearance. The top is flat and appears to be made of the same wood as the sides, tightly sealed. On one side, there is a small metal spigot or tap, suggesting the barrel is designed to dispense liquids. The spigot is affixed through one of the staves and is made of metal, contrasting with the wood. The overall look is utilitarian and robust, suitable for storage or dispensing of liquids in an urban or semi-industrial setting.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops and a side-mounted metal spigot, featuring a rustic, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The barrel is shown with its wooden staves and both metal hoops visible, with the spigot protruding from the right side.
description_view_1: The spigot is centered and fully visible, highlighting its function as a dispensing mechanism.
description_view_2: The spigot is visible on the left side, and the wood grain and metal hoops are clearly seen.
description_view_3: The barrel is shown from the opposite side of the spigot, displaying only the wooden staves and metal hoops with no spigot visible.
